title: Prognostic accuracy of age-adapted SOFA, SIRS, PELOD-2, and qSOFA for in-hospital mortality among children with suspected infection admitted to the intensive care unit

sample_size.source_quote: A final cohort of 2594 paediatric ICU admission encounters due to infection with known outcomes were identified, with a median age of 13 years (IQR 1–16).

patient_age_mean.source_quote: A final cohort of 2594 paediatric ICU admission encounters due to infection with known outcomes were identified, with a median age of 13 years (IQR 1–16).

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: 151 (5.8%) children died in hospital and 949 (36.6%) died in hospital or experienced an ICU length of stay of 3 days or more

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Baseline risk model using available information at ICU admission not contained in any of the scores
mortality_predictors.source_quote: discrimination was highest for SOFA (AUROC = 0.829) which was significantly higher than SIRS (AUROC = 0.727, p < 0.001), severe sepsis (AUROC = 0.711, p < 0.001), and qSOFA (AUROC = 0.739, p < 0.001)

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Baseline risk model using available information at ICU admission not contained in any of the scores
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The discrimination of outcomes was significantly higher for SOFA (adjusted AUROC 0.829; 0.791–0.868) and PELOD-2 (0.816; 0.777–0.854) than for qSOFA (0.739; 0.695–0.784) and SIRS (0.710; 0.664–0.756).

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Baseline risk model using available information at ICU admission not contained in any of the scores
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The discrimination of outcomes was significantly higher for SOFA (adjusted AUROC 0.829; 0.791–0.868) and PELOD-2 (0.816; 0.777–0.854) than for qSOFA (0.739; 0.695–0.784) and SIRS (0.710; 0.664–0.756).

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Baseline risk model using available information at ICU admission not contained in any of the scores
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The discrimination of outcomes was significantly higher for SOFA (adjusted AUROC 0.829; 0.791–0.868) and PELOD-2 (0.816; 0.777–0.854) than for qSOFA (0.739; 0.695–0.784) and SIRS (0.710; 0.664–0.756).

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Baseline risk model using available information at ICU admission not contained in any of the scores
Baseline risk model using available information at ICU admission not contained in any of the scores
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The best binary performance for PELOD-2 was using a cutpoint score of ≥ 8, resulting in an adjusted AUROC for in-hospital mortality of 0.812 (95% CI 0.774–0.851), and a sensitivity of 88.1% and a specificity of 55.7%.

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Baseline risk model using available information at ICU admission not contained in any of the scores

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Paediatric SIRS (≥2 SIRS criteria, one of which must be abnormal temperature or WCC)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Baseline risk model using available information at ICU admission
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Using models adjusted for baseline risk, the relative odds of death increased in the presence of paediatric SIRS (OR 1.83, 95% CI 1.13–2.99, p = 0.015)

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Number of SIRS criteria (each additional criterion)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: each additional SIRS criterion was associated with a 50% increase in the relative odds for the primary outcome (OR 1.50, 95% CI 1.25–1.81, p < 0.001)

mortality_predictors.outcome_definition: Secondary outcome: in-hospital mortality or ICU length of stay ≥3 days
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Baseline risk model using available information at ICU admission not contained in any of the scores
mortality_predictors.source_quote: For the secondary outcome, discrimination was highest for PELOD-2 (AUROC = 0.771), which was significantly higher than SIRS (AUROC = 0.676, p < 0.001), severe sepsis (AUROC = 0.677, p < 0.001), qSOFA (AUROC = 0.682, p < 0.001), and SOFA (AUROC = 0.751, p < 0.001)
